source: Limitless (Bankless) — AI supercycle; Unity as world-model builder; chips/memory/power
summary: Micron is a core beneficiary of AI memory demand, with HBM supply constraints driving extraordinary margins
summary_extended: Baker argues that Micron (MU) is a core portfolio holding and direct beneficiary of severe supply constraints in the AI memory market, particularly in High Bandwidth Memory (HBM). He cites SK Hynix's ~70% operating margins as evidence of the extraordinary pricing power that HBM scarcity confers on suppliers, and notes that hyperscalers like Google and Microsoft are reportedly offering $50–100 billion to lock in future HBM supply — underscoring how critical and constrained this resource is. Baker uses Micron's recent crossing of a $1 trillion market cap milestone as a data point illustrating the market's recognition of this structural dynamic. The investment implication is that Micron, as one of only three global HBM producers, benefits from the same supply-constraint tailwind as SK Hynix, making it a high-conviction long in the AI infrastructure buildout.
detail: Micron is held as a major position. The AI memory market is severely supply-constrained; SK Hynix (the leading HBM supplier) has ~70% operating margins and is fielding $50-100B offers from Google and Microsoft to lock in future supply. Micron benefits from the same structural dynamic.
confidence_evidence: Named as a significant portfolio holding; memory supply constraint thesis is central to Baker's anti-bubble argument
quote_fragment: Micron, one of the largest memory makers, just eclipsed a trillion dollars

ticker_audit.reasoning: Micron is explicitly named as a major portfolio holding and the direct subject of this thesis about HBM supply constraints and AI memory demand.
SK Hynix is referenced as evidence for the HBM margin/supply-constraint thesis, but it is not US-listed and is not the investment subject — it is used as a comparable to support the MU thesis.
Samsung is not explicitly discussed in this thesis excerpt and is not US-listed; including it as an implied ticker is not warranted by the specific claims made.
